criteria_checked.note: Independently recomputed shasum -a 256 of the plan == d0156d70e85d6c8d39a23e463c99da944b79228131f2431ff81d476f3fbf4caf, matching the claimed Rev 4 hash. Re-binds APPROVE to the current bytes; the r3 verdict (plan_hash e905ca9c...) is superseded. repo_hash kept at bbeb277 (plan's declared base); HEAD is presently the pre-amend local build commit 8451cc0.

The defect is REAL and VERIFIED at source: dmc-agents-md.py:174 sets name = os.path.basename(os.path.abspath(root)); committed AGENTS.md line 1 is literally '# AGENTS.md — DMC'. So a naive regen via --root ROOT inherits the checkout dir basename, and the positive byte-compare fails in ANY checkout dir != DMC. In CI the checkout is Do-Me-Coding, so the BLOCKING m65-suite step would go RED on push. The replica evidence (1c1 < # AGENTS.md — DMC from a replica-v114 dir) is exactly consistent. Live-tree lanes (dir DMC) structurally could not observe it — the AC-4 committed-replica leg is what caught it pre-push, validating replica-based verification (prove-the-work / no-fake-completion working as designed).

Confirmed the change set is EXACTLY the 5 disclosed edits and nothing else. (1) Proposed Changes suite description gains the name-pinned $TMP/DMC regen path + rationale (~155-162); (2) new Finding F8 with replica evidence + generator source ref dmc-agents-md.py:173 + CI Do-Me-Coding consequence (~109-120); (3) the environment-coupled-flake Risk row broadened to dirty-tree OR checkout-dir-name (~249); (4) AC criterion 1 gains a rename-decoupling verification line (~211-213), AC #4 UNCHANGED; (5) Notes Rev 4 line (~324). Byte-consistent with r3: F1-F7, Relevant Files (same 3-path lock), Out of Scope (generator still out of scope), assertions (1)-(5), AC criteria 2-7, Assumptions, T001-T003, and the entire Verification Commands table.

Correctness/scope lens. The $TMP/DMC name-pin is the correct root-cause layer and does NOT weaken the pin. It normalizes ONLY the checkout-dir-name environment artifact (line-1 basename); the regen still reflects full working-tree CONTENT (all sections, landmarks, commands), so a real committed-vs-regen drift still fails byte-for-byte. Alternatives correctly rejected: generator edit (dmc-agents-md.py is out of scope AND would change output for every host repo); compare-minus-line-1 (masking — blinds the pin to real title drift); clone-of-HEAD (stale HEAD bytes + reintroduces the tree coupling F6 forbids). Fail-safe: a change to the generator title LOGIC still produces a regen != committed => caught, not masked.

Security/hermeticity lens. cp -R of the working tree (incl .git) into mktemp $TMP/DMC keeps the suite hermetic: all writes confined to mktemp, the real repo only READ, the porcelain before/after DELTA (assertion 5) unaffected. Copying .git is REQUIRED for git check-ignore landmark-walk parity (without it, gitignored files could be walked -> spurious drift); the copied .git equals the real one, so it adds NO new environment coupling — it REMOVES the dir-name coupling. Non-blocking minor: copying full .git is a per-run cost, acceptable at DMC's size; no network/secret/live access introduced.

Scope/governance lens. Amending unpushed local commit 8451cc0 is governance-clean: verified it is HEAD of claude/dmc-fable-core with parent bbeb277 and NO remote contains it (git branch -r --contains 8451cc0 empty). Amending unshared local history is the clean way to fold a pre-push fix into the single logical change commit (Constitution anti-patchwork discipline prefers this over a fixup on a shipped surface). Both failure records preserved (G2 run dmc-run-e8b6a347af41 verified real in r3; the AC-4 replica catch recorded in F8 + Notes Rev 4); push never happened; LOCAL-commit autonomy ceiling honored. Post-amend the build evidence should record the final SHA.
